description_long: This sculpture depicts a highly detailed, life-sized human hand emerging vertically from a flat base, with fingers dramatically curled and reaching upward. The hand is anatomically realistic, showing defined knuckles, veins, and subtle creases in the skin, giving it a lifelike and expressive quality. The surface has a matte, stone-like texture with visible roughness, suggesting it is crafted from a dense material such as cast stone or concrete. The coloration is a consistent dark gray, with slight variations that enhance the sense of depth and realism. The base is a simple rectangular slab, lightly speckled, providing stability and contrast to the dynamic form of the hand. This striking piece would serve as a focal point in an urban park, plaza, or public walkway, inviting contemplation and interaction.
description: A realistic, life-sized sculpture of a human hand with curled fingers, emerging from a rectangular base and finished in dark gray stone-like material. The piece is highly detailed, with a rough matte surface and expressive anatomical features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the palm and curled fingers reaching upward, with the thumb and index finger most prominent.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the hand, knuckles, and wrist, with all five fingers visible in silhouette.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the length of the hand and the dramatic curl of the fingers, with the thumb partially obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the thumb extended outward and the curvature of the palm, with the base clearly visible.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is placed on a flat, speckled surface, suggesting an outdoor urban or park setting.
